meta.title: Verbal Reasoning Test
meta.description: 500 objective questions assessing comprehension, vocabulary, critical analysis, and related verbal skills.

questions.prompt: According to the passage, what did the learner do each day?
questions.visual: A learner wanted to improve reading fluency. Each day, the learner read aloud for twenty minutes. After three weeks, the learner read more smoothly.
options: rehearsed in front of a mirror
solved short sums without a calculator
questions.explanation: The passage directly states that the learner read aloud for twenty minutes.

questions.prompt: Which sentence best summarises the passage?
questions.visual: A learner wanted to improve reading fluency. Each day, the learner read aloud for twenty minutes. After three weeks, the learner read more smoothly.
options: The learner stopped practising reading fluency.
The learner mastered reading fluency in one day.
Practice had no effect on reading fluency.
Regular practice improved the learner’s reading fluency.
questions.explanation: The passage links repeated practice with improvement.

questions.prompt: What can reasonably be inferred from the passage?
questions.visual: A learner wanted to improve reading fluency. Each day, the learner read aloud for twenty minutes. After three weeks, the learner read more smoothly.
options: The learner disliked every practice session.
No time was spent on reading fluency.
Consistent effort probably contributed to better reading fluency.
The improvement happened before practice began.
questions.explanation: The timing supports a cautious link between steady practice and improvement.

Review the attempt → notice improvement → set a goal → asked a partner for feedback
